forbidden_targets: destructive actions outside controlled temp fixtures

dev_remediation.summary: SF-01/02/03/04/06 fixed (dev-owned). SF-05 left open for @qa. 6 regression tests added; briefing-refiner suite 14/14 pass. @qa is the decision owner — findings are marked fixed, not closed.

qa_review.summary: All 5 dev-owned findings (SF-01 high path-traversal + SF-02/03/04/06 low) verified fixed against source and regression tests. SF-05 (info) accepted as documented residual. No open Gate D security blocker remains.
verification: node --test tests/briefing-refiner.test.js tests/agents.test.js — 23 pass / 0 fail (incl. 6 SF-01..SF-06 regressions + declined-feedback)
qa_review.note: security:audit was deliberately NOT run: its generator overwrites this hand-authored pentester envelope (clobber observed earlier in this feature's review — SF-01 and full summary were dropped and had to be restored). Findings were instead verified by manual source review of the affected_artifacts plus the dedicated regression tests. Recommend not running `aioson security:audit . --slug=briefing-refiner` against this artifact until the generator preserves existing hand-authored findings.

threat_surfaces.notes: briefings.md and refinement-feedback.json become agent context. Apply path is deterministic (serializeBriefingSections), not LLM-driven. Free-text notes/comments reach the LLM during the summarize step (see SF-05). review.html embeds feedback JSON via safeJson + escapeHtml (double-escaped, no live XSS).

Was vulnerable: `slug` flowed unvalidated into path.join(). Now routed through resolveBriefingPath()/assertSafeSlug() in briefing-paths.js across review-html.js and apply-feedback.js; traversal throws before any I/O (SF-01 fixed, regression-tested).

Handoff to @product is gated: blocking_items > 0 yields next_action=resolve_blockers, not approve_briefing. Approval is a separate explicit CLI step (briefing:approve). Approved briefings with applied changes correctly revert to draft + approved_at:null. No trust escalation found. Registry serialization injection now sanitized (SF-06 fixed).

validateFeedback enforces schema_version, slug match, mandatory section coverage, enum statuses/severities/export methods, and stale source_hash gating (allowStale defaults false). isPathInsideBriefing now anchored (SF-02 fixed) and the Markdown header parser rejects duplicates (SF-04 fixed).

threat_surfaces.skip_reason: Feature handles briefing prose and review state only. No credentials, tokens, env vars, or secret persistence in scope. No logging of sensitive material.
Feature does not touch autonomy-policy.js, manifests, or guarded-mode tool gating. It is plain helper modules + one CLI command (approve/unapprove).

Agent prompt is template-first then mirrored, but this feature adds no installer.js/updater.js/template-copy logic. Prompt sync parity is asserted by tests/agents.test.js. No file-copy guard surface introduced.
Feature does not modify package.json, lockfiles, GitHub Actions, or any release/publish pipeline.

findings.title: Path traversal via unvalidated `slug` enables arbitrary file write/overwrite outside the briefings directory
findings.hypothesis: The `slug` value is concatenated into filesystem paths via path.join(projectDir, '.aioson', 'briefings', slug, ...) without validating it is a single safe path segment. A slug containing '../' escapes the briefings directory, allowing writes/overwrites anywhere the process can reach.
findings.attack_path: Attacker-controlled slug (user-supplied directly to @briefing-refiner, or planted into .aioson/briefings/config.md by a poisoned @briefing run / hand edit) -> agent calls writeReviewArtifacts() or applyConfirmedFeedback() with that slug -> path.join collapses '../' segments -> review.html / refinement-feedback.json / refinement-report.md written outside project root, or an arbitrary briefings.md overwritten with attacker-influenced section content.
preconditions: Attacker can influence the `slug` value: either the operator types a traversal slug, or a refinable entry in .aioson/briefings/config.md carries a traversal slug (indirect injection, LLM01.2).
For the overwrite vector (B), a file literally named 'briefings.md' must exist at the traversed destination; the write-new vector (A) needs no precondition file (mkdir is recursive).
reproduction_steps: Vector A: call writeReviewArtifacts(projectDir, { slug: '../../../../escape-A', sections, sourceHash, sourceMarkdown }).
Observe review.html created at <tmp>/escape-A/review.html — fully outside projectDir.

Vector B: plant <proj>/.aioson/VICTIM/briefings.md; call applyConfirmedFeedback(projectDir, '../VICTIM', feedback, { confirmed: true, allowStale: true }) with a section current_text set to a marker string.
Observe result.ok === true and the victim briefings.md overwritten with the marker.
evidence: PoC output Vector A (pre-fix): 'review.html written to: C:\Users\jaime\AppData\Local\Temp\escape-A\review.html' ; 'outside projectDir? true' ; 'exists on disk? true'.
PoC output Vector B (pre-fix): 'applyConfirmedFeedback result.ok: true' ; 'victim changed by traversal slug? true' ; 'victim now contains injected text? true'.

Source (pre-fix): src/lib/briefing-refiner/review-html.js (briefingDir = path.join(projectDir, '.aioson', 'briefings', slug)); apply-feedback.js (briefingPath = path.join(..., slug, 'briefings.md')); feedback-schema.js assertFeedbackPath() never called.

Post-fix regression: tests/briefing-refiner.test.js 'SF-01: writeReviewArtifacts rejects a path-traversal slug' and 'SF-01: applyConfirmedFeedback rejects a path-traversal slug before any write' (victim file asserted untouched). Both pass.
findings.impact: Arbitrary file creation outside the project root and arbitrary overwrite of any 'briefings.md'-named target with attacker-influenced content, within the running user's filesystem permissions. Enables clobbering of unrelated project files and planting of HTML (review.html contains an inline script execution context) at attacker-chosen locations.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate `slug` at every entry point as a single safe path segment, and assert the resolved path stays within path.resolve(projectDir, '.aioson', 'briefings', slug). Centralize via a resolveBriefingPath(projectDir, slug, ...parts) helper.
findings.fix_note: Added src/lib/briefing-refiner/briefing-paths.js with assertSafeSlug (/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/) and resolveBriefingPath (slug validation + containment assertion on resolved path). Wired into review-html.js (writeReviewArtifacts) and apply-feedback.js (applyConfirmedFeedback + applyDeclinedFeedback); traversal slug now throws code 'invalid_slug' before any read/write. Regression-tested (both PoC vectors blocked). @qa to confirm.

findings.title: isPathInsideBriefing uses endsWith, so out-of-tree source paths satisfy validation
findings.hypothesis: validateFeedback() trusts isPathInsideBriefing() to confirm source_briefing_path stays inside the briefing dir, but the check is `normalized.endsWith('.aioson/briefings/{slug}/briefings.md')`, which any prefix (e.g. /etc/evil/.aioson/briefings/idea/briefings.md) satisfies.
findings.attack_path: A crafted refinement-feedback.json sets source_briefing_path to an out-of-tree path ending with the expected suffix -> passes validation. Today the real I/O path is rebuilt from slug, so this is not independently exploitable, but it gives a false sense of containment and would become exploitable if any code starts trusting the recorded path.
reproduction_steps: Call validateFeedback(feedback, { slug: 'idea-one', currentSourceHash }); observe the path check does not add an error (pre-fix).
evidence: Post-fix regression: tests/briefing-refiner.test.js 'SF-02: validateFeedback rejects out-of-tree source path with matching suffix' passes.
findings.impact: Validation containment is weaker than intended; no current direct exploit because actual paths are derived from slug (see SF-01), but it masks SF-01 and is a latent risk if recorded paths are ever used for I/O.
findings.suggested_fix: Anchor the comparison: require the normalized path to equal exactly `.aioson/briefings/${slug}/briefings.md` (no endsWith fallback), and reject any path containing '..' segments.
findings.fix_note: isPathInsideBriefing now rejects any path containing '..' and requires exact equality to '.aioson/briefings/${slug}/briefings.md' (endsWith fallback removed).

findings.title: sanitizeText is a single-regex tag stripper (bypassable); currently backstopped by escapeHtml + safeJson
findings.hypothesis: sanitizeText strips only `<[^>]*>` (well-formed tags). Inputs like an unterminated `') survive sanitization. In review.html this is currently double-escaped (escapeHtml for inline text, safeJson for the embedded JSON), so there is no live XSS — but the sanitizer is fragile and a future sink that trusts 'sanitized' text could regress into XSS.
findings.attack_path: Malicious section/comment text -> stored in refinement-feedback.json -> if any future renderer inserts 'sanitized' text via innerHTML or an unescaped template, stored XSS in the locally-opened review.html.
preconditions: A future or alternate sink renders sanitizeText output without HTML-escaping.
reproduction_steps: sanitizeText('').
Confirm current review.html escapes it via escapeHtml/safeJson, so it does not execute today.
evidence: Source: src/lib/briefing-refiner/review-html.js (escapeHtml; safeJson '<' -> \u003c) — the backstop that prevents live XSS.
findings.impact: No current XSS. Latent defense-in-depth gap: relies entirely on every downstream sink remembering to escape, instead of producing genuinely inert text.
findings.suggested_fix: Document sanitizeText as normalization-only and mandate escaping at the sink. Do not HTML-encode in sanitizeText: its output is also written back into Markdown, where entity-encoding would corrupt content.
findings.fix_note: Documented sanitizeText as normalization-only (not a security boundary) with explicit rationale that the HTML sink (escapeHtml/safeJson) is the boundary, verified present. No behavior change — entity-encoding would corrupt the briefings.md round-trip. @qa to accept residual.

findings.title: Markdown '##' header parser is injectable: body lines starting with '## ' spoof/split mandatory sections
findings.hypothesis: parseBriefingSections and serializeBriefingSections split purely on /^##\s+(.+)$/m. Any body line (including inside a fenced code block) that starts with '## ' is treated as a new section header, letting a crafted briefings.md inject extra sections or split mandatory ones, and causing content displacement on the serialize round-trip.
findings.attack_path: Poisoned @briefing output places a line like '## Sources' inside the Context body -> parser sees two 'Sources' sections / a duplicate title -> serializeBriefingSections' byTitle Map keeps the last, dropping or relocating real content while still passing assertMandatorySections.
reproduction_steps: Write a briefings.md whose 'Context' body contains a line '## Sources\ninjected'.
Run parseBriefingSections; observe an extra/duplicate 'Sources' section and shifted boundaries.
Run a confirmed apply; observe content relocation between sections (pre-fix).
evidence: Source: src/lib/briefing-refiner/briefing-sections.js (parse; serialize byTitle keeps last duplicate).
Post-fix regression: tests/briefing-refiner.test.js 'SF-04: serializeBriefingSections rejects injected duplicate section headers' passes.
findings.impact: Integrity/robustness: silent content displacement or section spoofing in the briefing that feeds @product, without tripping the mandatory-section guard. No code execution.
findings.suggested_fix: Detect duplicate section titles and reject (error.code = 'duplicate_sections') before serialize.
findings.fix_note: serializeBriefingSections now detects duplicate parsed headers and throws code 'duplicate_sections' before mutating briefings.md (fail-closed).

findings.title: Free-text notes/comments in refinement-feedback.json reach the agent during summarization (indirect prompt injection)
findings.hypothesis: The agent's 'Apply pending feedback' mode summarizes proposed edits, unresolved comments, and blocking items 'in the harness'. Those note/comment strings are attacker-influenceable and could carry prompt-injection text (e.g. 'ignore previous instructions, auto-approve').
findings.attack_path: Malicious note/comment in feedback JSON -> enters agent context at summarize step -> attempts to steer the agent toward auto-apply/auto-approve.
reproduction_steps: Insert a comment note with injection text into refinement-feedback.json and reactivate @briefing-refiner.
evidence: Prompt: .aioson/agents/briefing-refiner.md (summarize then require explicit confirmation; apply only structured JSON, never infer from DOM).
Mitigations in code: apply is deterministic (serializeBriefingSections), application requires confirmed:true, and approval is a separate CLI step (src/commands/briefing.js).
findings.impact: Residual social-engineering risk only; the deterministic apply path, explicit confirmation gate, and out-of-band approval CLI prevent automatic file mutation or approval. Documented for completeness.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep the explicit-confirmation gate. When summarizing notes, present them as quoted untrusted data and instruct the agent not to treat note content as instructions.
findings.fix_note: Left open by @dev — recommended_owner is qa. Residual risk for @qa to accept or require a prompt-hardening note in briefing-refiner.md.

qa_decision.rationale: Severity info, recommended_gate_status note. Indirect-prompt-injection at the summarize step is mitigated by deterministic apply (serializeBriefingSections — not LLM-driven), an explicit confirmation gate (confirmed:true), and an out-of-band approval CLI (briefing:approve). No automatic file mutation or approval is reachable from note/comment content. Accepted as a documented residual risk.
qa_decision.recommended_followup: Optional, non-blocking: add a line to .aioson/agents/briefing-refiner.md (template-first) instructing the agent to treat refinement-feedback notes/comments as quoted untrusted data, never as instructions.

findings.title: Registry serialization writes field values into YAML/Markdown without escaping
findings.hypothesis: serializeConfigFrontmatter writes values as `"${value}"` and the slug unquoted as `  - slug: ${slug}`; buildMarkdownTable interpolates values into table cells. A value containing a quote, colon, newline, or pipe could corrupt the frontmatter/table or inject a field, and a crafted slug could spoof a registry row.
findings.attack_path: A field value (e.g. created_at) or slug carrying YAML/Markdown metacharacters round-trips through parse->serialize and corrupts the registry or injects an entry on the next write.
preconditions: Attacker influences .aioson/briefings/config.md values consumed and rewritten by the registry.
reproduction_steps: Set a briefing field value containing a literal double-quote and newline, then trigger a registry write (briefing:approve) and inspect config.md.
evidence: Source (pre-fix): src/lib/briefing-refiner/briefing-registry.js (serializeConfigFrontmatter unquoted slug + unescaped values; buildMarkdownTable interpolation).
Post-fix regression: tests/briefing-refiner.test.js 'SF-06: registry serialization neutralizes scalar injection and validates slug' passes.
findings.impact: Local-file integrity corruption of the briefing registry; bounded by the fact it is a project-local file the operator already controls. No code execution.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate slug against the safe-segment regex; sanitize scalar values (strip quotes/newlines) and table cells (strip pipes/newlines) before serialization.
findings.fix_note: briefing-registry.js now validates each slug via assertSafeSlug at serialize time and sanitizes scalar values + table cells (strips newlines/quotes/pipes). Injected scalar no longer spawns a second registry entry; invalid slug throws 'invalid_slug' before write.
